Joshua was old and dying, so listen carefully to what he said. He reminded them of the great things God had done for them during his lifetime. He urged them to be very brave. Carefully observe, diligently do, and sincerely regard what is written. Also, be very cautious that pagan idolatry may be forgotten, so it will never return. It is sad that among Christians the names of pagan gods are so commonly used and familiar. Joshua urges them to remain steadfast. There might be many problems among them, but they had not abandoned the Lord their God; the way to improve people is to make the best of them.

If we want to stay loyal to the Lord, we must always be careful, because many people are lost through carelessness. Love the Lord your God, and you will not abandon him. Has God been so true to you? Do not be false to him. He is faithful who has promised. The experience of every Christian confirms this truth. Conflicts may have been severe and long, trials great and numerous; but in the end he will acknowledge that goodness and mercy followed him all the days of his life. Joshua states the disastrous consequences of turning back; know for certain it will be your ruin. The first step would be friendship with pagans; the next would be marrying them; the final result would be serving their gods. This is how the path of sin goes downhill, and those who have fellowship with sinners cannot avoid having fellowship with sin. He describes the destruction he warns them about. The goodness of the heavenly Canaan, and the free and certain promise God has made of it, will increase the misery of those who will forever be shut out from it. Nothing will make them see how wretched they are so much as to see how happy they could have been. Let us watch and pray against temptation. Let us trust in God’s faithfulness, love, and power; let us ask for his promises, and stay loyal to his commands, then we will be happy in life, in death, and forever.
